steps.title: Identify the noun requiring agreement
steps.content: The blank is in the phrase “of ______ nurses,” where the word filling it must modify the plural noun “nurses.”

steps.title: Apply the demonstrative agreement rule
steps.content: When a demonstrative pronoun modifies a noun, it must agree with the noun in number. “These” is plural and correctly modifies “nurses”; the other three are either singular or don't function as demonstrative modifiers in this position. That matches choice D — D is correct.
